start.speaker: fenrick
start.text: He glances over both shoulders, then pulls you into the shadow of the tannery wall. His voice drops to something you've never heard from him before -- sincerity.

check_clear.speaker: fenrick
check_clear.text: You asked about the cargo. I shouldn't tell you this. But if you're going to judge me, at least judge the real me.

the_truth.speaker: fenrick
the_truth.text: Every thirty years, when the Cycle comes, children die first. Always. The weak, the small, the ones nobody protects. The Guild has been smuggling orphans and at-risk children out of Ashvale through our trade routes for three generations. Not for profit. For survival.

the_cost.speaker: fenrick
the_cost.text: Do you know how much it costs to feed and house forty children in a town that doesn't want them? The Guild's 'greed' pays for beds, bread, and passage east. Every coin I squeeze from a drunk merchant or a fat lord goes to keeping some kid alive in a village that forgot their name.

the_weight.speaker: fenrick
the_weight.text: He leans against the wall, and for a moment the mask drops entirely. He looks tired. Not the performative exhaustion of a man who wants sympathy -- the bone-deep fatigue of someone carrying a weight they never asked for.

the_weight_2.speaker: fenrick
the_weight_2.text: My predecessor started it. Old Harlan. Found three kids starving in a root cellar during the last Cycle. Couldn't save their parents, but he got the children out. Hid them in a grain shipment heading east. After that... it became tradition. The one good thing the Hand does that nobody knows about.

options.text: I'll help. What do you need?
This changes nothing. You're still a criminal.
The Guild's vault heist from the manor -- that was about the children too?

alliance_path.speaker: fenrick
alliance_path.text: He stares at you. Searching for the lie. For the angle. Finding neither, something shifts behind his eyes -- a door opening that he'd bricked shut years ago.

alliance_path_2.speaker: fenrick
alliance_path_2.text: The eastern route is compromised. Ironmarch patrols have been pushing west, and our usual contacts in Millhaven have gone quiet. I need a new safe house, or fourteen children are trapped in Ashvale when the Cycle peaks. If you can find us a way east -- any way -- I'll owe you more than gold can repay.

judged_path.speaker: fenrick
judged_path.text: The mask slides back into place. Smooth. Practiced. But you saw what was underneath, and you both know it.

judged_path_2.speaker: fenrick
judged_path_2.text: Fine. I'm a criminal. And those fourteen 'crimes' are sleeping in a cellar right now, warm and fed, because a criminal gave a damn when nobody else did. Get out of my sight.

vault_reveal.speaker: fenrick
vault_reveal.text: His eyes widen. You can see him recalculating you -- not as a mark, not as a threat, but as someone who actually pays attention.

vault_reveal_2.speaker: fenrick
vault_reveal_2.text: The vault doesn't hold gold. It holds records. Every family consumed by the pact over three hundred years -- names, bloodlines, last known relatives. If we can get those records, we can find the children's surviving kin. Give them a real home, not just a hiding place. That's what the heist was always about.

children_count.speaker: fenrick
children_count.text: This generation? Fourteen. The youngest is three. She still calls for her mother at night. Her mother was the first to fall when the wells went bad.
